title: Implement shader-based vertex displacement
summary: Replace the sine-hash layeredNoise() in both vertex shaders with true 3D simplex noise FBM for sculptural audio-reactive deformation. Existing displacement layers (radial expansion, twist, directional drift, treble jitter, pointer repulsion, breathing scale) are preserved — only the slow-modulation section and bass/treble displacement scaling are augmented with coherent 3D noise. No new VisualParams fields; displacement intensity is derived from existing motionAmplitude and structureComplexity at uniform-update time.

steps.title: Create shared simplex 3D noise GLSL chunk
steps.description: Write src/visual/shaders/simplex3d.glsl containing a self-contained snoise(vec3) implementation (Ashima/webgl-noise style, no texture lookups). Include an fbm3(vec3, int octaves) wrapper with lacunarity=2.0 and gain=0.5. This file will be imported as a raw string and prepended to vertex shaders via string concatenation (Vite ?raw import).
steps.tests: Verify the GLSL compiles by checking shader material creation in existing test pattern.
steps.risk: Low — pure addition, no existing code modified.

steps.title: Upgrade pointWarp.vert.glsl with simplex noise displacement
steps.description:
Modify the vertex shader to use 3D simplex noise for sculptural deformation. All existing displacement layers are PRESERVED as-is:

- Radial expansion (line ~68-70): KEPT — bass still pushes points outward radially
- Twist around Y (lines ~72-80): KEPT — bass-driven rotational twist
- Directional drift (lines ~82-86): KEPT — sin/cos per-axis movement
- Treble micro-jitter (lines ~88-92): KEPT — high-frequency fine displacement
- Pointer repulsion (lines ~103-111): KEPT — screen-space push
- Field spread and breathing (lines ~114-117): KEPT

CHANGES — two targeted modifications:

1. **Replace layeredNoise() function** (lines 31-40) and its calls in the slow-modulation section (lines 94-101) with fbm3() from the prepended simplex3d.glsl chunk. The slow-modulation section currently calls layeredNoise(pos.x * nf + t*0.0001, pos.y * nf, pos.z * nf) — replace with fbm3(pos * uNoiseFrequency + vec3(t * 0.00005), uNoiseOctaves) for coherent 3D spatial deformation instead of oscillatory sine-hash patterns.

2. **Add bass-driven macro noise layer** as a NEW displacement step (after existing radial expansion, before twist). Sample fbm3(pos * 0.5 + vec3(uTime * 0.00003), max(uNoiseOctaves, 1)) and displace along normalize(pos) scaled by uBassEnergy * uDisplacementScale * 0.3. This gives bass a sculptural, volumetric push in addition to the existing radial expansion.

New uniforms to add: uDisplacementScale (float). No uNoiseEvolutionSpeed — reuse cadence-driven uTime scaling instead.
steps.tests: Verify new uniform uDisplacementScale exists on shader material. Verify shader compiles (no WebGL errors). Verify vertex shader string contains 'snoise'.
steps.risk: Medium — core visual change. Test with bass=0 and bass=1 to confirm displacement range is reasonable.

steps.title: Upgrade ribbonWarp.vert.glsl with simplex noise displacement
steps.description: Mirror step 2 changes for the ribbon shader in src/visual/shaders/ribbonWarp.vert.glsl and src/visual/systems/ribbonField.ts. Same approach: preserve all existing displacement layers (radial expansion, ribbon-specific bass sway, twist, drift, jitter, pointer repulsion, field spread, breathing). Replace only layeredNoise() function and its calls in the slow-modulation section with fbm3(). Add the same bass-driven macro noise layer. Retain the ribbon-specific sinusoidal sway (ribbonPhase, lines ~72-75) as an additive layer — it provides ribbon-unique character on top of the shared noise displacement. Add uDisplacementScale uniform.
steps.tests: Verify new uniform exists and shader compiles. Verify vertex shader string contains 'snoise'.
steps.risk: Medium — same as step 2 but for ribbon mode.

steps.title: Wire uDisplacementScale uniform from existing VisualParams
steps.description:
In both pointCloud.ts and ribbonField.ts draw() methods, compute uDisplacementScale as motionAmplitude * structureComplexity at uniform-update time. No new VisualParams fields needed — these existing params already encode the right signals:
- motionAmplitude = 0.2 for prefers-reduced-motion, 1.0 otherwise → displacement automatically calms
- structureComplexity = 0.2-1.0 from device DPR/cores → weaker devices get less displacement

Also import simplex3d.glsl as raw string (Vite ?raw) and prepend it to the vertex shader string before passing to ShaderMaterial constructor. Reuse cadence (already a uniform as uCadence) to modulate noise evolution speed in the shader — multiply the time offset in fbm3 sampling by uCadence so nighttime visits (lower cadence) get slower, more meditative deformation drift.
steps.tests: Verify uDisplacementScale updates when frame params change. Verify motionAmplitude=0.2 produces uDisplacementScale < 0.3.

steps.title: Quality tier scaling verification
steps.description: The existing noiseOctaves quality tier (1/2/3 from quality.ts) already controls fbm3 octave count via uNoiseOctaves. Verify that low-tier (1 octave simplex) still performs well with 200 points. If needed, scale uDisplacementScale *= 0.6 for low tier in draw() to prevent visual artifacts from single-octave noise being too uniform. No changes to quality.ts or VisualParams needed.
steps.tests: Verify low/medium/high quality tiers produce valid shader uniforms and compile cleanly.

steps.title: Write acceptance tests
steps.description:
Create tests/visual/shaderDisplacement.test.ts verifying all acceptance criteria:

1. ShaderMaterial used: Both pointCloud and ribbonField create THREE.ShaderMaterial
2. Bass affects large-scale 3D displacement: With bassEnergy=0.8, uBassEnergy and uDisplacementScale uniforms are set > 0
3. Treble affects fine-grain displacement: With trebleEnergy=0.7, uTrebleEnergy uniform is set > 0
4. Deformation evolves over time: draw() at elapsed=0 and elapsed=30000 produces different uTime values (noise field drifts)
5. No CPU per-point loops: draw() does NOT modify position buffer attributes (all deformation GPU-side)
6. Simplex noise chunk included: vertex shader source string contains 'snoise' function
steps.tests: Self — this step IS the acceptance test suite.

steps.title: Build verification and smoke test
steps.description:
Run npm run build to verify production build succeeds with the new GLSL imports. Run the full test suite. Verify in dev server that:
- Point cloud deforms fluidly with audio (sculptural, not oscillatory)
- Ribbon field deforms fluidly with audio
- Deformation is visibly 3D (depth/parallax in displacement)
- Scene works when muted (synthetic bass fallback still drives displacement)
- Reduced-motion mode calms displacement
- No console errors

acceptance_criteria_mapping.Geometry deformation occurs in a ShaderM: Already true from US-032/US-034. Steps 2-3 enhance the shader displacement with 3D simplex noise; step 6 test #1 verifies ShaderMaterial usage.
acceptance_criteria_mapping.Bass energy affects large-scale displace: Steps 2-3 add low-frequency simplex FBM displaced along radial direction, driven by uBassEnergy * uDisplacementScale. Existing radial expansion is preserved as additive layer. Step 6 test #2 verifies.
acceptance_criteria_mapping.Treble energy affects fine-grain displac: Existing treble jitter (sin/cos based) is preserved. Slow-modulation section upgraded to simplex noise adds coherent fine-grain variation. Step 6 test #3 verifies.
acceptance_criteria_mapping.Deformation evolves over time (not stati: Noise sampling coordinates drift via uTime * 0.00005 * uCadence, producing continuous sculptural evolution. Step 6 test #4 verifies.

constraints: No CPU-based per-point loops for deformation (ticket note)
All displacement computed GPU-side in vertex shaders
Must work with existing quality tier system (1/2/3 octaves)
Must degrade gracefully with prefers-reduced-motion (motionAmplitude scales uDisplacementScale)
No new npm dependencies — simplex noise is pure GLSL
No new VisualParams fields — derive displacement scale from existing motionAmplitude * structureComplexity
Must not break mute toggle, info overlay, or autoplay fallback
Preserve all existing displacement layers (radial expansion, twist, drift, jitter, pointer repulsion, breathing)

key_decisions.decision: Use Ashima-style GLSL simplex noise (no texture lookups)
key_decisions.rationale: Self-contained, no 3D texture setup needed, well-tested in WebGL community, compatible with WebGL 1 and 2.

key_decisions.decision: Prepend simplex3d.glsl via string concatenation rather than #include
key_decisions.rationale: Vite raw imports handle this cleanly. Three.js ShaderMaterial doesn't support GLSL #include natively without onBeforeCompile hooks.

key_decisions.decision: Derive uDisplacementScale from motionAmplitude * structureComplexity inline in draw()
key_decisions.rationale: Avoids expanding VisualParams for a derived value. motionAmplitude already respects prefers-reduced-motion, structureComplexity scales with device capability.

key_decisions.decision: Reuse uCadence for noise evolution speed modulation
key_decisions.rationale: cadence (0.4-1.0) represents time-of-day modulation speed. Nighttime visits get slower, more meditative deformation drift — aligned with artistic intent. No new uniform needed.

key_decisions.decision: Preserve all existing sin/cos displacement layers, only replace layeredNoise() calls
key_decisions.rationale: Existing radial expansion, twist, drift, jitter, pointer repulsion, and breathing layers already provide good base motion. The sine-hash layeredNoise() in the slow-modulation section is the only part that produces non-sculptural oscillatory patterns. Replacing just that with 3D simplex noise achieves the sculptural goal without regressing existing visual character.
